basis: The full rank-two character lattice is a GL2Z local system wherever the integral torus exists.
Every independent co-shortest pair is unimodular.
Determinant magnitude one gives index one, not a proper submodule.
The two members cease to be simultaneously shortest in either adjacent chamber.
A vertex supplies three shortest lines and three unimodular pair choices.
A metric-derived co-shortest pair requires the branch to remain on a two-way wall and its cocycles to preserve the unordered splitting; otherwise only a supplied continuation exists.
The stabilizer consists exactly of signed permutation matrices.
Ordering/sign changes conjugate the Gram matrix; trace, determinant, and absolute angle survive.
K=w1 tensor w1+w2 tensor w2 is sign/order independent once the pair reduction exists.
b=W^T S transforms as the pair representation; it is not a phase section.
db transforms in the same pair representation on locally constant lattice charts.
Generic GL2Z monodromy preserves Lambda* but not the unordered pair.
J swaps the pair and has diagonal/anti-diagonal eigenlattices.
-J swaps the same unoriented pair and is conjugate to J by a sign basis change.
Order-four swap lifts square to -I and have no nonzero fixed lattice.
The fixed lattice of J is Z(1,1).
The anti-fixed lattice is Z(1,-1).
Fixed plus anti-fixed eigenlattices span an index-two sublattice.
A global involutive exchange exponentiates its primitive fixed lattice to a circle.
(1,-1) annihilates the J-fixed diagonal circle, up to sign/basis conjugacy.
Normalized reciprocal coframe weights give amplitudes with ratio exp(2phi), but the amplitude interpretation and phases remain supplied.
Independent primitive caps give the known determinant-one toric S3 only in the supplied completion.
J and -J fixed circles have weights (1,1) or (1,-1), free on supplied S3.
The free fixed-circle action gives a smooth quotient only after FC04 global data.
Diagonal and anti-diagonal actions give opposite signs and the same absolute unit class.
A principal-circle quotient or S2 base is not a selected S2-valued field.
The rank-two orbit lattice exists off a cap; at the cap an isotropy quotient replaces it.
A nonintegrable plane field has no global character module.
Common positive scale does not choose a pair member, lift, cap, or quotient.
No action, source, density law, mass, scale, or time evolution follows.
